rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR. VS. RECOLOGY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, Apr-14-2025, Line 7. DEFENDANTS RECOLOGY, INC., GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL AND RECYCLING COMPANY, RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO, INC., and MARCUS MIRT'S Motion For An Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security. 1. Plaintiff shall furnish the security as to Defendants Recology, Inc., Golden Gate Disposal and Recycling Company, Recology San Francisco, Inc., and Marcus J. Mirt by April 28, 2025 2. The court orders Plaintiff to show cause that he has done so, or to show cause why the matter should not be dismissed pursuant to CCP 391.4, on May 6, 2025, at 9 a.m. in Department 301 of this court. 3. Plaintiff may file his response to the order to show cause five court days in advance of the hearing or may personally appear to show cause. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: JAVIER E. MARTINEZ VS. GENERAL MOTORS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, Apr-14-2025, Line 4. 1-PLAINTIFF JAVIER MARTINEZ', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set Two. Motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 1and 4-13/Granted. 1. The interrogatories at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Plaintiff has shown Defendant's responses are incomplete. 2. Defendant has not demonstrated that any objection has merit. 3. The Court orders Defendant to provide further responses to the cited special interrogatories. Plaintiff's meet and confer was reasonable. 4. Defendant must provide verified responses no later than ten court days from notice of this order. There is no basis for issue sanctions. 5. The Court finds Defendant acted without substantial justification. Accordingly, Defendant shall pay $3,450 to Plaintiff in sanctions. Payment is to be made within 10 days of service of order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH ET AL VS. SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS, DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, Apr-14-2025, Line 1. 3-DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS', DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Demurrer to the First Amended Complaint/Overruled. 1. Per CCP 452, a court liberally construes the pleadings. 2. The use of the term "DEFENDANTS" does not render the First Amended Complaint uncertain. 3. "Demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond." (A.J. Fistes Corp. v. GDI. Best Contractors, Inc. (2019) 38 Cal.App.5th 677, 695.) 4. Sutter can reasonably respond and raises factual issues for discovery. The court notes that plaintiffs allege when decedent was in Sutter's custody and when she developed an ulcer there. (FAC, pars. 9, 11-13, and 19.) The court further finds that causes of action two for willful misconduct and three for elder abuse are alleged with sufficient particularity. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH ET AL VS. SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS, DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, Apr-14-2025, Line 1. 2-DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS', DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER, MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Motion to strike/Denied. 1. The prayer for attorney's fees is proper based on the elder abuse claim. (See Welfare & Institutions Code sec. 15657(a).) 2. The prayer for punitive damages without compliance with CCP 425.13 is also proper based on the elder abuse claim. (Covenant Care, Inc. v. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 790 ["Where the gravamen of an action is violation of the Elder Abuse Act, Central Pathology's rationale for applying section 425.13 to the common law intentional torts at issue in that case does not obtain."].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH ET AL VS. SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS, DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, Apr-14-2025, Line 1. 1-DEFENDANTS LAKEPORT POST ACUTE, LLC, PROVIDENCE GROUP NH, LLC, PACS HOLDINGS, LLC, and PACS GROUP INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Motion to compel arbitration/denied. 1. CCP 1295 and Ruiz v. Podolsky (2010) 50 Cal.4th 838 [compelling non signatories to arbitrate medical malpractice claim] do not apply to this case because the gravamen of plaintiffs' claims is elder abuse/neglect. 2. "If the primary basis for the wrongful death claim sounds in professional negligence as defined by MICRA, then section 1295 applies. If, as plaintiffs claim here, the primary basis is under the Elder Abuse and Dependent Adult Civil Protection Act (Welf. & Inst. Code, Sec. 15600 et seq.) (the Act), then section 1295 does not apply and neither does Ruiz 's exception to the general rule that one who has not consented cannot be compelled to arbitrate." (Avila v. Southern California Specialty Care, Inc. (2018) 20 Cal.App.5th 835, 842.) Plaintiffs (heirs) never agreed to arbitrate their claims. 3. CCP 1281.2(c) [conflicting rulings] applies to this action since the gravamen of the claims is elder abuse. The court denies arbitration of decedent's survivor claims because there is a possibility of conflicting rulings with plaintiffs' wrongful death claims and their claims against Sutter Bay Hospitals, who is not required to arbitrate. (CCP 1281.2(d); Daniel v. Sunrise Senior Living, Inc. (2013) 212 Cal.App.4th 674, 686 [denying arbitration where there was a possibility of conflicting rulings between the survivor and wrongful death claims].) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WILLIAM DRAKE VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery/Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, Apr-14-2025, Line 10. PLAINTIFF PAUL DRAKE'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions. 1. Plaintiffs' motion to compel the deposition of the defendant's person most knowledgeable and production of documents/Granted. 2. Defendant must produce the PMK and all-nonprivileged responsive documents by April by 25, 2025. 3. If any documents are withheld on the grounds of privilege, the withheld documents must be identified in a privilege log. 4. The discovery sought by plaintiffs is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and the defendant's boilerplate objections lack merit. 5. Plaintiff's "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects is proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].) 6. Defendant did not present any competent evidence showing that the requests are unduly burdensome. 7. Defendant did not act with substantial justification and shall pay $2,000 in sanctions to Plaintiff within 10 days of service of service of order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/BZ)
